strategy: Verify the overall last smartphone-use moment across the full video, then inspect the anchored phone-screen frame sequence and localized UI region to identify the highlighted option as directly as possible.

steps.purpose: Find all candidate moments in the full video where the guy is visibly using or holding a smartphone, so the final occurrence can be grounded globally rather than only within the prior 70-74s local sequence.

inputs.query: all moments where the guy visibly uses or holds a smartphone, especially taking a photo or interacting with the phone screen

expected_outputs.clips: candidate smartphone-use intervals across the whole video with the latest occurrence included

steps.purpose: Retrieve a chronological local sequence around the latest grounded smartphone-use interval to verify the final smartphone-use moment and preserve before/after context.

inputs.query: chronological frames showing the final smartphone-use moment and immediate neighbors, including the phone screen
time_hints: around 74s if that remains the latest candidate, but prefer the latest smartphone-use interval returned by grounding

expected_outputs.frames: chronological frames around the globally last smartphone-use moment

steps.purpose: Localize the smartphone screen region and the highlighted bottom-right/thumbnail-like UI element in the final smartphone-use frames so the answer-critical UI can be inspected directly.

inputs.query: the smartphone screen and the highlighted onscreen option or selected thumbnail/gallery button on that screen

expected_outputs.regions: localized phone-screen and highlighted UI regions
expected_outputs.spatial_description: description of where the highlighted option appears on the phone screen

steps.purpose: Attempt to read any visible label or text associated with the highlighted onscreen option from the localized phone-screen region.

inputs.query: read any visible text or label on or near the highlighted onscreen option

expected_outputs.text: OCR text from the highlighted phone-screen UI region
expected_outputs.lines: line-level OCR results for any readable option text

steps.purpose: Using the verified final smartphone-use sequence, localized UI description, OCR output, and preserved prior evidence, determine which answer option is highlighted and whether 74s is indeed the overall last smartphone-use moment.

inputs.query: From these chronological frames, determine whether this is the overall last time the guy uses his smartphone in the video. Then identify the highlighted onscreen option at that moment. Prefer direct readable text from OCR if present; otherwise use the localized UI appearance and standard camera/gallery semantics only if the visual evidence clearly supports one option over the others. Explicitly reject unsupported alternatives.

expected_outputs.answer: final option mapping with explicit support for the last-use anchor and highlighted option
expected_outputs.supporting_points: key observations about chronology, UI location, OCR text if any, and option elimination
expected_outputs.analysis: reasoned justification with uncertainty handling if the label remains unreadable

refinement_instructions: Preserve the prior 70-74s candidate and prior evidence IDs, but do not treat the earlier icon-to-option mapping as sufficient proof. First establish whether the latest smartphone-use clip returned by grounding still ends at the same moment as the prior 74s candidate; if a later smartphone-use interval appears, use that later interval instead. In the trace, explicitly separate two claims: (1) why the chosen moment is the overall last smartphone use in the video, and (2) what onscreen option is highlighted at that moment. Prefer OCR-backed label text if any is readable. If OCR yields no usable text, rely on the localized UI region plus chronological frames to identify the highlighted control, and explain why that control matches one option better than the others. Do not claim direct readable option text unless OCR actually provides it. If the UI remains too small or ambiguous, keep the answer cautious and note that the support is icon-based rather than text-based.
